structure.description: Detail footer surfaces a publish toggle + URL preview + 'last published' timestamp when entity is published. Publish creates a PublishedX shadow record (not a copy of the entity) carrying URL + timestamp. Unpublish is irreversible (URL may be cached/shared) — surfaces irreversible-confirm pattern. Distinct from generic visibility because it produces an external artifact (URL).

rationale.hypothesis: Per-item publish is a deliberate consent moment — not all content in a vault should be public. A footer toggle (vs. catalog-level filter or workspace-level setting) keeps the consent local to the item being published. The fact that PublishedNote is its own entity (not a flag on Note) means publish-state survives note edits and exposes URL/timestamp as first-class.

evidence.description: Per-note checkbox in publish-plugin pane, URL preview, last-published time
Per-page 'Share to web' toggle with URL
Per-post publish toggle as the canonical CMS pattern

counterexample.description: Sharing is permission-based, not publish-as-static-artifact — different mental model

shouldMatch.reason: Page has share-to-web semantics; PublishedPage shadow entity fits

shouldNotMatch.reason: Publishing controls would violate confidentiality invariants
